title: PAIK Common Frontmatter
description: Fields shared by every PAIK document, plus the reusable owner/link building blocks every document draws on. See SPEC.md section 2.

owner.description: Who is responsible for this document's subject. Inline by design - PAIK has no separate team/roster document type; the org's own directory (HR system, chat admin, Backstage) stays the source of truth for who is actually on a team.

name.description: Team or individual name, e.g. "Orders team"

ref.description: Optional link to where this owner is documented in the org's own systems (a wiki page, an org chart) - never a list of personal emails/handles here

link.description: A single typed pointer to an external system or resource. `kind` is the only enforced key, on purpose - one shape covers ticketing, wikis, API specs, repos, external services, secrets locations, dashboards, runbooks, on-call, and anything else. See SPEC.md section 3 for common `kind` values. Fields beyond `kind` are intentionally open (unlike the rest of a PAIK document), since the whole point of `links` is not needing a new schema per system.

kind.description: What this link points at, e.g. repository, api, jira-project, jira-epic, jira-component, confluence, external-service, secrets, dashboard, runbook, pagerduty, status-page, deploy-pipeline, chat, other

id.description: The identifier this link's system uses internally, e.g. a Jira project key or component key

purpose.description: Free-text disambiguator when a kind can appear more than once on the same document, e.g. purpose: architecture on a second confluence link

provider.description: The vendor/product behind this link, e.g. jira, confluence, github, gitlab, swaggerhub, pagerduty, vault

paik.description: PAIK spec version this document conforms to

id.description: Stable kebab-case slug, unique within the project

extensions.description: Free-form, namespaced fields for org-specific needs that don't belong in the core schema, e.g. "company.hu/data-owner": "finance". Namespace keys as domain/key to avoid collisions; this is intentionally not validated further.
